hints.Nominative: While 'Rex' has the same form in nominative and vocative, the context of address makes it vocative
hints.Genitive: Genitive of 'rex' would be 'regis' — 'Rex' here is a form of direct address
hints.Accusative: Accusative of 'rex' would be 'regem' — 'Rex' here is vocative

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'regis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'regis' ends in -is
hints.4th declension: 4th declension nouns have genitive in -ūs — 'regis' ends in -is

hints.Nominative: Nominative is 'maiestas' — 'maiestatis' has the -is genitive ending
hints.Dative: Dative of 'maiestas' would be 'maiestati' — 'maiestatis' ends in -is (genitive)
hints.Ablative: Ablative of 'maiestas' would be 'maiestate' — 'maiestatis' ends in -is (genitive)

hints.1st declension: 1st declension nouns have genitive in -ae — 'maiestatis' ends in -is
hints.2nd declension: 2nd declension nouns have genitive in -i — 'maiestatis' ends in -is
hints.5th declension: 5th declension nouns have genitive in -ei — 'maiestatis' ends in -is

hints.Present subjunctive: Present subjunctive 2nd sg. of 'salvare' would be 'salves' — 'salvas' has the -a- indicative stem
hints.Perfect indicative: Perfect 2nd sg. would be 'salvavisti' — 'salvas' is present tense
hints.Future indicative: Future 2nd sg. would be 'salvabis' — 'salvas' is present tense

hints.saluto, salutare, salutavi, salutatum: These are the principal parts of 'salutare' (to greet)
hints.sano, sanare, sanavi, sanatum: These are the principal parts of 'sanare' (to heal)
hints.libero, liberare, liberavi, liberatum: These are the principal parts of 'liberare' (to free)

hints.Present participle: Present participle accusative plural would be 'salvantes' — 'salvandos' has the -nd- gerundive marker
hints.Perfect participle: Perfect participle would be 'salvatos' — 'salvandos' has the -nd- gerundive marker
hints.Future participle: Future participle would be 'salvaturos' — 'salvandos' has the -nd- gerundive marker

translation: King of fearsome majesty, who saves freely those who must be saved
